claimText: Ideonella sakaiensis is a species of bacteria capable of degrading Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET) plastic.
evidenceExcerpt: Bacteria Ideonella sakaiensis: Plastic eating bacteria that can degrade PET (Polyethylene Terephthalate)
examUse: Correct-pair question matching organisms to their bioremediation functions.

claimText: Polybutylene Adipate Terephthalate (PBAT) is a biodegradable thermoplastic copolymer derived from renewable resources like corn starch and sugarcane.
evidenceExcerpt: Polybutylene Adipate Terephthalate (PBAT): Biodegradable, chemical-free thermoplastic copolymer made from renewable resources, like corn starch and sugarcane
examUse: Statement-based question on biodegradable plastics and their sources.

claimText: Phthalates are chemical plasticizers added to plastics like polyvinyl chloride (PVC) to make them softer and less brittle.
evidenceExcerpt: Pthalates: Ortho-phthalates, or “phthalates,” are chemicals used in plastic products (commonly in specific type of plastic named polyvinyl chloride) to make the material soft and less brittle
examUse: Statement-based question on the chemical properties and uses of plastic additives.
distractorHooks: Used to make plastics harder and more rigid

claimText: Phthalates are categorized as hazardous endocrine-disrupting chemicals (EDCs) that negatively affect reproductive health.
evidenceExcerpt: Categorized as hazardous endocrine-disrupting chemicals (EDCs); affect reproductive health.
examUse: Statement-based question on the health and environmental impacts of plasticizers.
